name: USS Leutze (DD 481)
events: 28 Mar 1945 At 1148 I/28, in the Philippine Sea, in position 20°48'N, 131°34'E, USS Burrfish (T/Lt.Cdr. M.H. Lytle, USN) sighted a destroyer and two destroyer escorts through high periscope.

This were the destroyer USS Leutze (T/Lt. L. Grabowsky, USN) and the escort destroyers USS Whitehurst (Lt. J.C. Horton, USNR) and USS England (Lt. J.A. Williamson, USNR) en-route from the Okinawa area to Ulithi. (1)
